ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or virtual location of the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the server */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the server */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the server */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the server record */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant owning the server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server the metrics are collected from */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metrics were recorded */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Amount of memory usage in MB */,
  "disk_io_operations" REAL /* Number of disk I/O operations per second */,
  "network_throughput" REAL /* Network throughput in Mbps */,
  "memory_free" REAL /* Amount of free memory in MB */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage in GB */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency in milliseconds */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* GPU usage percentage (if applicable) */,
  "gpu_memory_usage" REAL /* GPU memory usage in MB (if applicable) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the metric record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server associated with the alert */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., CPU, Memory) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value to trigger the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "email_notifications" INTEGER /* Indicator if email notifications are enabled */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., absolute, percentage) */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration for which the threshold must be breached to trigger the alert */,
  "last_triggered" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last time the alert was triggered */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant for which the alert is configured */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES servers ("tenant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system (e.g., admin, engineer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicator if the user account is active */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant to which the user belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES servers ("tenant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant for which the access log is recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES servers ("tenant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_allocations" (
  "allocation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource allocation entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server to which resources are allocated */,
  "cpu_allocated" REAL /* Amount of CPU allocated (in cores) */,
  "memory_allocated" REAL /* Amount of memory allocated (in GB) */,
  "storage_allocated" REAL /* Amount of storage allocated (in GB) */,
  "gpu_allocated" REAL /* Amount of GPU allocated (in cores) */,
  "network_bandwidth_allocated" REAL /* Amount of network bandwidth allocated (in Mbps) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the allocation record */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant for which the resources are allocated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allocation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocations_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocations_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES servers ("tenant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert triggering the notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Time when the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "sent_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who sent the notification */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tenant for which the notification is sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES servers ("tenant_id")
)
